NAME

Detailed Description

WebSocket server implementation.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file lib/network/websocket/server.c.

Function Documentation

void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t * server)

Wake the WebSocket server event loop immediately. Thread-safe. Wakes lws_service() from its timeout wait, allowing the event loop to check the running flag without waiting for the full service timeout (50ms).

Parameters

server Server instance (must have a valid context)

Definition at line 827 of file lib/network/websocket/server.c.

827                                                                  {
828   if (server && server->context) {
829     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
830   }
831 }




References websocket_server::context.


Referenced by server_main().






void
  websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t * server)


Destroy WebSocket server and free resources.


Parameters


server Server instance to destroy


Definition at line 833 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


833                                                           {
834   if (!server) {
835     return;
836   }
837 
838   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, false);
839 
840   // Cancel any pending libwebsockets service calls to interrupt blocking lws_service()
841   // This prevents the event loop from getting stuck in lws_service()
842   if (server->context) {
843     log_debug("[WEBSOCKET_SERVER_DESTROY] Cancelling libwebsockets service");
844     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
845   }
846 
847   // Unregister atomic fields
848   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&server->running);
849 
850   // Destroy handler thread pool (waits for pending work to complete)
851   if (server->handler_pool) {
852     thread_pool_destroy(server->handler_pool);
853     server->handler_pool = NULL;
854   }
855 
856   // Context is normally destroyed by websocket_server_run (from the event loop
857   // thread) for fast shutdown. This handles the case where run() wasn't called
858   // or didn't complete normally.
859   if (server->context) {
860     NAMED_UNREGISTER(server->context);
861     log_debug("WebSocket context still alive in destroy, cleaning up");
862     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
863     server->context = NULL;
864   }
865 
866   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
867 }






References atomic_store_bool(),
    websocket_server::context, websocket_server::handler_pool,
    log_debug, NAMED_UNREGISTER, websocket_server::running,
    and thread_pool_destroy().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t websocket_server_init
  (websocket_server_t * server, const websocket_server_config_t *
  config)


Initialize WebSocket server. Creates libwebsockets context and
    prepares for accepting connections.




Parameters


server Server instance to initialize


Config Module Server configuration


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 677 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


677                                                                                                              {
678   if (!server || !config || !config->client_handler) {
679     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
680   }
681 
682   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
683   server->handler = config->client_handler;
684   server->user_data = config->user_data;
685   server->port = config->port;
686   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, true);
687 
688   // Store server pointer in protocol user data so callbacks can access it
689   websocket_protocols[0].user = server; // http protocol
690   websocket_protocols[1].user = server; // acip protocol
691 
692   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
693   lws_log_init_server();
694 
695   // Configure libwebsockets context
696   struct lws_context_creation_info info = {0};
697   info.port = config->port;
698   info.protocols = websocket_protocols;
699   info.gid = (gid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
700   info.uid = (uid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
701   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT; // Initialize SSL/TLS support (required for server binding)
702   info.extensions = NULL;                              // Disable permessage-deflate - causing connection issues
703   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &keep_alive_policy; // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
704 
705   // Configure TLS/WSS support if certificates are provided (check for non-empty strings)
706   if (config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0' && config->tls_key_path &&
707       config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0') {
708 #ifdef LWS_WITH_TLS
709     // Validate certificate and key files are readable
710     if (access(config->tls_cert_path, R_OK) != 0) {
711       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS certificate file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_cert_path,
712                        errno);
713     }
714     if (access(config->tls_key_path, R_OK) != 0) {
715       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS key file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_key_path, errno);
716     }
717 
718     info.ssl_cert_filepath = config->tls_cert_path;
719     info.ssl_private_key_filepath = config->tls_key_path;
720     log_info("WebSocket server configured for WSS (TLS): cert=%s, key=%s", config->tls_cert_path, config->tls_key_path);
721 #else
722     log_warn("WebSocket server: TLS support not compiled in libwebsockets; WSS unavailable");
723 #endif
724   } else if ((config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0') ||
725              (config->tls_key_path && config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0')) {
726     log_warn("WebSocket server: Both TLS certificate and key must be provided for WSS; using plain WS");
727   }
728 
729   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
730   // Default is 4KB, causing large frames to fragment into many chunks
731   // Video frames are 921KB, so increase to 2MB to handle them without fragmentation
732   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 2 * 1024 * 1024; // 2MB per-thread service buffer
733 
734   // Disable ALL default timeouts and keep-alive mechanisms
735   // Use only the explicit retry_and_idle_policy we set above (30/35 seconds)
736   info.ka_time = 0;           // Disable TCP keep-alive probes (use WebSocket pings instead)
737   info.ka_probes = 0;         // Disable TCP probes
738   info.ka_interval = 0;       // Disable TCP probe intervals
739   info.keepalive_timeout = 0; // Disable HTTP keep-alive timeout entirely
740 
741   // Explicitly set a very large idle timeout to prevent early disconnection
742   // Without this, libwebsockets defaults to 5 seconds for HTTP connections
743   // Set to effectively infinite (100 hours) since we use retry_and_idle_policy instead
744   info.timeout_secs = 360000; // 100 hours - effectively disabled
745 
746   // Create libwebsockets context
747   // Log diagnostic info before attempting creation
748   log_debug("lws_create_context: port=%u, protocols=%p, options=0x%x", info.port, (void *)info.protocols, info.options);
749 
750   server->context = lws_create_context(&info);
751   if (!server->context) {
752     // Capture any OpenSSL errors that may have occurred during context creation
753     unsigned long ssl_err = 0;
754     char ssl_err_str[256] = "no OpenSSL errors in queue";
755 
756     // Extract all errors from OpenSSL error queue
757     while ((ssl_err = ERR_get_error()) != 0) {
758       ERR_error_string_n(ssl_err, ssl_err_str, sizeof(ssl_err_str));
759       log_error("OpenSSL error: %lu: %s", ssl_err, ssl_err_str);
760     }
761 
762     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "Failed to create libwebsockets context (last OpenSSL error: %s)",
763                      ssl_err_str);
764   }
765 
766   // Create thread pool for handling client connections
767   // Use 4 worker threads to handle concurrent client handlers without blocking LWS event loop
768   server->handler_pool = thread_pool_create_with_workers("websocket_handlers", 4);
769   if (!server->handler_pool) {
770     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
771     server->context = NULL;
772     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create WebSocket handler thread pool");
773   }
774 
775   /* Register WebSocket server with named registry */
776   char ws_port_name[32];
777   snprintf(ws_port_name, sizeof(ws_port_name), "ws:%d", server->port);
778   NAMED_REGISTER(server, ws_port_name, "websocket_server", "0x%tx", NULL);
779 
780   /* Register server's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
781   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&server->running, "is_running", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
782 
783   /* Register WebSocket context as child of server */
784   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET(server->context, "context", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
785 
786   log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d with static file serving", config->port);
787   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
788 }

    websocket_server_config_t::client_handler,
    websocket_server::context, errno, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, ERROR_THREAD,
    websocket_server::handler, websocket_server::handler_pool,
    log_debug, log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    lws_log_init_server(), NAMED_REGISTER,
    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET,
    websocket_server::port, websocket_server_config_t::port,
    websocket_server::running, SET_ERRNO,
    thread_pool_create_with_workers(),
    websocket_server_config_t::tls_cert_path,
    websocket_server_config_t::tls_key_path,
    websocket_server::user_data, and
    websocket_server_config_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t websocket_server_run
  (websocket_server_t * server)


Run WebSocket server event loop. Blocks until server is signaled
    to stop via running flag.




Parameters


server Server instance


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 790 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


790                                                                    {
791   if (!server || !server->context) {
792     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid server");
793   }
794 
795   log_info("WebSocket server starting event loop on port %d", server->port);
796 
797   // Run libwebsockets event loop
798   uint64_t last_service_ns = 0;
799   int service_call_count = 0;
800   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->running)) {
801     // Service libwebsockets with 16ms timeout to match 60 FPS frame rate.
802     // This provides frequent event processing (~60 callback invocations per second) so that
803     // WebSocket frames queued by the render loop are sent promptly, matching TCP performance.
804     // All client connections share this single server context, so a single lws_service() call
805     // services fragments and events for all clients simultaneously.
806     uint64_t service_start_ns = time_get_ns();
807     if (last_service_ns && service_start_ns - last_service_ns > 30 * US_PER_MS_INT) {
808       // > 30ms gap between service calls
809       double gap_ms = (double)(service_start_ns - last_service_ns) / 1e6;
810       log_info_every(1 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE_GAP] %.1fms gap between lws_service calls", gap_ms);
811     }
812     service_call_count++;
813     log_debug_every(500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE] Call #%d, context=%s", service_call_count,
814                     NAMED_DESCRIBE(server->context, "websocket_server"));
815     int result = lws_service(server->context, 1);
816     if (result < 0) {
817       log_error("libwebsockets service error: %d", result);
818       break;
819     }
820   }
821 
822   log_info(
823       "WebSocket server event loop exited (context will be destroyed by main thread after handler threads complete)");
824   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
825 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    websocket_server::context, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    log_debug_every, log_error, log_info,
    log_info_every, NAMED_DESCRIBE, websocket_server::port,
    websocket_server::running, SET_ERRNO, time_get_ns(),
    and US_PER_MS_INT.
